content.full_text:
January 26, 1979

Jonathan Brown
926 J Street, #719
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Brown:

Thank you for your letter of January 23, 1979. You stated that
you intend to monitor and report on legislation for a particular
client but you will not attempt to influence legislative or
administrative action on behalf of the client. You asked if
you must add this client to your lobbyist registration statement.

A lobbyist must register and be authorized by any person or
organization which directly employs, contracts for, or retains
the lobbyist to influence or attempt to influence legislative
or administrative action. Accordingly, you are not required to
amend your registration statement to add a client for whom you
are only monitoring and reporting on legislation.

If I can be of further assistance please contact me.

Sincerely,


Richard Eichman
Compliance Representative

RE:bw

JONATHAN BROWN

January 23, 1979

Mr. Richard Eichman
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Rich,

I've been contacted by a friend in another
state who would like me to follow legislation for
his association's membership. They've asked me
to keep them informed on legislative developments.
The services they've requested would be very limited.
I would report to them on pending legislation which
would affect their membership. The contract would
entail no direct contact with members of the Legi-
slature or covered employees in the executive branch.

While it is clear to me that I would be required
to register if the association requested me to in-
crease my contacts in their behalf with members, it
is not clear what my legal responsibilities are if
I maintain a limited role.

Assuming that I signed an agreement which re-
stricted my services to monitoring and reporting
on legislation would I be required to add them to
my registration?

Thank you for any clarification you can supply.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Brown

Sacramento Office
926 J Street, #719
Sacramento, CA. 95814

sections.question: Assuming that I signed an agreement which restricted my services to monitoring and reporting on legislation would I be required to add them to my registration?
sections.conclusion: A lobbyist must register and be authorized by any person or organization which directly employees, contracts for, or retains the lobbyist to influence or attempt to influence legislative or administrative action. Accordingly, you are not required to amend your registration statement to add a client for whom you are only monitoring and reporting on legislation.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a lobbyist register a client when the engagement is limited to monitoring and reporting on legislation with no direct contact with legislators or executive branch officials?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A lobbyist is not required to register a client if the engagement is limited to monitoring and reporting on legislation without attempting to influence legislative or administrative action.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Clear Q&A format with incoming request (Brown to FPPC) followed by FPPC response. Document contains both the original request letter and the FPPC's formal response letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Assuming that I signed an agreement which restricted my services to monitoring and reporting on legislation would I be required to add them to my registration?

CONCLUSION: A lobbyist must register and be authorized by any person or organization which directly employees, contracts for, or retains the lobbyist to influence or attempt to influence legislative or administrative action. Accordingly, you are not required to amend your registration statement to add a client for whom you are only monitoring and reporting on legislation.

You asked if you must add this client to your lobbyist registration statement. A lobbyist must register and be authorized by any person or organization which directly employees, contracts for, or retains the lobbyist to influence or attempt to influence legislative or administrative action. Accordingly, you are not required to amend your registration statement to add a client for whom you are only monitoring and reporting on legislation. Sincerely, Richard Eichman Compliance Representative JONATHAN BROWN January 23, 1979 Mr. Richard Eichman Fair Political Practices Commission 1100 K Street P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 Dear Rich, I've been contacted by a friend in another state who would like me to follow legislation for his association's membership. They've asked me to keep them informed on legislative developments. The services they've requested would be very limited. I would report to them on pending legislation which would affect their membership. The contract would entail no direct contact with members of the Legislature or covered employees in the executive branch. While it is clear to me that I would be required to register if the association requested me to increase my contacts in their behalf with members, it is not clear what my legal responsibilities are if I maintain a limited role. Assuming that I signed an agreement which restricted my services to monitoring and reporting on legislation would I be required to add them to my registration? Thank you for any clarification you can supply. Sincerely, Jonathan Brown Sacramento Office 926 J Street, #719 Sacramento, CA. 95814
embedding.summary: FPPC Compliance Representative Richard Eichman advises Jonathan Brown that he is not required to register a client for whom he only monitors and reports on legislation, as registration is required only when the lobbyist attempts to influence legislative or administrative action.
